title: ENTRUSTINC_07_24_1998-EX-10.5-STRATEGIC ALLIANCE AGREEMENT
answer: Yes, the contract limits liability in several ways: it excludes indirect, incidental, consequential, special, and punitive damages (with exceptions for Article VIII and Article XII), caps general liability between Nortel and Entrust at U.S.$5,000,000 (with a U.S.$10,000,000 cap for breaches of Article II), and limits ETI's intellectual property indemnity liability to 50% of monies paid by Nortel up to a maximum of U.S.$1,000,000. Additionally, the disclosing Party has no liability for information disclosed under the Agreement.

citations.text: Except for breach of Article                -------------------------------                               VIII and for Article XII, in no event shall either Party be liable to the other Party for any indirect, incidental and/or consequential damages resulting from a breach of this agreement, including without limitation lost business, lost savings, and lost profits even if the breaching Party has been advised of the possibility of the occurrence of such damages.
In no event shall either Party be liable for any special or punitive damages arising from breach of this Agreement.
Notwithstanding the foregoing, each of Nortel's and Entrust's liability to the other Party for breach of Article II shall not exceed U.S.$10,000,000.

ETI's liability to Nortel arising from or relating to the intellectual     property indemnity set forth in the Enterprise License shall not exceed 50%     (fifty percent) of the monies paid by Nortel thereunder to a maximum of     U.S.$1,000,000 (one million U.S. dollars).

The disclosing Party makes no                ----------------------                                representations, does not warrant, and shall have no liability whatsoever in respect of any information disclosed by it pursuant to this Agreement.

title: ENTRUSTINC_07_24_1998-EX-10.5-STRATEGIC ALLIANCE AGREEMENT
answer: The provided passages do not state whether any liability is left uncapped; they only specify monetary limits of U.S.$5,000,000 for general causes of action and U.S.$10,000,000 for breaches of Article II.

citations.text: For any cause of action arising under this                ---------------                                             Agreement, Nortel's liability to Entrust, and Entrust's liability to Nortel shall not exceed U.S.$5,000,000.
Notwithstanding the foregoing, each of Nortel's and Entrust's liability to the other Party for breach of Article II shall not exceed U.S.$10,000,000.

citations.text: This Promotion Agreement ("Agreement")  is entered into as of February 3, 2010 ("Effective Date")  by and between  MiddleBrook Pharmaceuticals, Inc. ("MBRK") ,  a Delaware corporation with offices at 7 Village Circle, Suite 100,  Westlake, TX 76262 and DoctorDirectory.com, Inc.  ("DD") ,  a South Carolina Corporation, with offices at One Page  Avenue, Suite 280, Asheville, NC 28801.

title: MIDDLEBROOKPHARMACEUTICALS,INC_03_18_2010-EX-10.1-PROMOTION AGREEMENT
answer: The initial term of the contract expires three (3) years from the Promotion Commencement Date.

citations.text: This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect for an initial term of  three (3) years from the Promotion Commencement Date, divided into three one-year periods.

title: MIDDLEBROOKPHARMACEUTICALS,INC_03_18_2010-EX-10.1-PROMOTION AGREEMENT
answer: Yes, assigning the agreement or any part of it to a third party requires the prior written consent of the other party, although such consent cannot be unreasonably withheld.

citations.text: neither party shall have  the right to assign this Agreement or any part thereof to a third party without the prior written consent of the other party,  however such consent will not be unreasonably withheld.

title: MIDDLEBROOKPHARMACEUTICALS,INC_03_18_2010-EX-10.1-PROMOTION AGREEMENT
answer: The provided passages do not mention a cap or limit on a party's liability, although they do state that parties are excused from liability for failures or delays in performance caused by force majeure events.

citations.text:
Each party shall be excused from liability for the failure or delay in performance of any obligation under this Agreement  by reason of any extraordinary, unexpected and/or unavoidable event, such as acts of God, fire, flood, explosion,  earthquake, or other natural forces, war, civil unrest, accident, destruction or other casualty, any lack or failure of  transportation facilities, any lack or







failure of supply of raw materials, any strike or labor disturbance, or any other event similar to those enumerated above.

title: ELANDIAINTERNATIONALINC_04_25_2007-EX-10.21-Outsourcing Agreement
answer: The parties to the contract are Bank of South Pacific Ltd (referred to as BSP) and Datec (also referred to as Datec PNG Ltd).

citations.text: Bank of South Pacific Ltd   Network Management Outsourcing Agreement
Document outline prepared by Des Kearse: Datec PNG Ltd May 2004
BSP is formally engaging Datec to be the network manager for the provision of service for the day-to-day operations of the wide area network (WAN) service.

title: ELANDIAINTERNATIONALINC_04_25_2007-EX-10.21-Outsourcing Agreement
answer: Yes, both parties may terminate the contract for convenience. The BSP may terminate the whole or any part of the agreement for convenience by providing at least 6 months prior written notice, and Datec may similarly terminate the whole or any part of the agreement for convenience by providing at least 6 months prior written notice.

citations.text: The BSP may terminate the whole or any part of this agreement for convenience at any time by giving Datec at least 6 months prior written notice.
Datec may terminate the whole or any part of this agreement for convenience at any time by giving the BSP at least 6 months prior written notice.

("Sandler O'Neill" or the "Agent")

title: ALLIANCEBANCORPINCOFPENNSYLVANIA_10_18_2006-EX-1.2-AGENCY AGREEMENT
answer: The contract limits liability in two ways: first, it limits the indemnifying parties' liability for fees and expenses to no more than one counsel (and one local counsel per jurisdiction) separate from their own for related actions; second, it states that the Agent shall have no liability (direct, indirect, in contract, tort, or otherwise) to the MHCs and its members, the Bank, the Company's, the MHCs's or the Bank's creditors regarding the Agent's engagement or performance.

citations.text: In no event shall the indemnifying parties be liable for fees and expenses of more than one counsel (in addition to no more than one local counsel in each separate jurisdiction in which any action or proceeding is commenced) separate from their own counsel for all indemnified parties in connection with any one action or separate but similar or related actions in the same jurisdiction arising out of the same general allegations or circumstances.

The Company, the MHCs and the Bank also agree that the Agent shall not have any liability (whether direct or indirect, in contract or tort or otherwise) to the MHCs and its members, the Bank, the Company's, the MHCs's or the Bank's creditors relating to or arising out of the engagement of the Agent pursuant to, or the performance by the A

citations.text: If any of the Securities remain available after the expiration of the Offerings, the Company agrees to offer the Agent the first right to act as lead managing underwriter for the Public Offering.
